question: Write a C++ program that prompts the user to enter their first name, last name, and age. Then, display a greeting message including all this information on the console. Ensure appropriate data types are used for each input.

test_cases.expected: Hello, Alice Wonderland, you are 25 years old.
Hello, John Paul Jones, you are 30 years old.
Hello, Bob Builder, you are 0 years old.

errors: Poorly chosen and uninformative variable names (n1, n2, a) hinder code readability.
Excessive and inefficient string concatenation for the final output message, creating multiple temporary string objects instead of streaming directly to `std::cout`.
Lack of comments to explain code sections and variable purposes.
Unnecessary extra newline output at the end of the program.
